summary: Markets advanced Friday as chipmakers surged on Intel's blowout earnings and hopes for US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan lifted sentiment. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed at record highs while the Dow slipped modestly, reflecting a divergence between tech and energy-sensitive sectors. Intel shares soared more than 20% after reporting stronger-than-expected Q1 results driven by AI data-center demand, igniting a rally in AMD (+12%) and across the semiconductor sector. Bitcoin ETFs extended their strongest inflow streak since October, pulling in $2 billion over eight consecutive days as institutional capital returned. Meanwhile, investors are looking ahead to the April 28-29 FOMC meeting where markets price a hold at 3.5%-3.75%, with traders focused on Powell's language around inflation and the path for rate cuts. Meta announced it will cut 10% of its workforce ahead of its April 29 earnings report, while mega-cap tech (Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta) reports Wednesday evening in a make-or-break test of AI monetization and capex spending. The mix of strong AI-driven growth, geopolitical optimism, and persistent inflation creates a backdrop where the Fed must balance data-dependency against ongoing uncertainty, setting the stage for heightened volatility around next week's policy decision and Big Tech earnings.

signals.title: Intel earnings spark historic chip rally on AI data-center demand
signals.desc: Intel reported Q1 beat with double-digit server CPU growth, sending shares up 21% and fueling AMD +12% surge as analysts upgrade on CPU bottleneck demand.

signals.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows hit $2B over 8-day streak, BlackRock IBIT now top 1% ETF
signals.desc: Spot Bitcoin ETFs logged their longest inflow streak since October 2025, with BlackRock's IBIT holding 806,700 BTC (3.8% of supply) and absorbing 91% of flows.

signals.title: FOMC meeting April 29 priced for hold, traders focus on Powell language
signals.desc: Markets price 99.7% probability of no change at 3.5%-3.75% range; focus shifts to inflation commentary and rate-cut timeline amid sticky CPI and oil near $96.

signals.title: Meta to cut 10% of workforce (8,000 employees) as AI capex surges
signals.desc: Meta confirmed layoffs ahead of April 29 earnings, as investors scrutinize whether $135B AI capex plan will translate to revenue growth amid disruption fears.

signals.title: US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan ease oil pressure, but risk remains elevated
signals.desc: Reports of Iranian foreign minister arriving in Pakistan for talks sent WTI down 2.4% to ~$94, but geopolitical uncertainty keeps Strait of Hormuz supply risk live.

verdict: Semiconductors lead markets to records on AI infrastructure demand while FOMC and mega-cap tech earnings next week will determine if the rally extends or pauses.
